title: pelargonium × dysgenon
intro: Pelargonium × dysgenon, also known as South African geranium, is a plant species that belongs to the Geraniaceae family and is a hybrid of the Pelargonium reniforme and Pelargonium citrinum species. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and anxiety due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × dysgenon include relief from digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve sleep quality. Pelargonium × dysgenon can be prepared in various forms, including tea, capsules, and essential oil, which can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
